hook.text: Write a value, read it from any node, always get the latest. That's strong consistency. Everyone sees the same state.

buildup.text: Strong consistency requires coordination. All replicas must agree before a read returns.

discovery.text: Google Spanner achieves strong consistency globally using synchronized atomic clocks (TrueTime).

twist.text: Strong consistency adds latency. Cross-region coordination means waiting for the slowest replica.

climax.text: Use strong consistency for critical data: balances, inventory counts, permission checks.

punchline.text: Strong consistency: always correct, but coordination costs latency.

quiz.question: How does Google Spanner achieve global strong consistency?
options: It uses synchronized atomic clocks (TrueTime) to order transactions across data centers
It stores all data in a single data center
It only allows one transaction at a time globally
